title: podman run SIGSEGV on git master in rootless mode
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I've been evaluating podman locally on my Debian buster laptop and I see a SIGSEGV when following through the "Running a sample container" instructions at https://podman.io/getting-started/getting-started.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Build and install podman on Debian buster according to https://podman.io/getting-started/installation (note: I did not build runc since this was already installed via `sudo apt-get install ...`)

2. Ensure you are currently logged in as a non-root user

3. Execute the command given in "Running a sample container" at https://podman.io/getting-started/getting-started

**Describe the results you received:**

I see a SIGSEGV with the following stacktrace:

```
$ podman run -dt -p 8080:8080/tcp -e HTTPD_VAR_RUN=/var/run/httpd -e HTTPD_MAIN_CONF_D_PATH=/etc/httpd/conf.d                   -e HTTPD_MAIN_CONF_PATH=/etc/httpd/conf                   -e HTTPD_CONTAINER_SCRIPTS_PATH=/usr/share/container-scripts/httpd/                   registry.fedoraproject.org/f27/httpd /usr/bin/run-httpd
ERRO[0002] could not find slirp4netns, the network namespace won't be configured: exec: "slirp4netns": executable file not found in $PATH 
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference

[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x0 pc=0x110c4fc]

        vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:864 +0x2b
main.main()
        cmd/podman/main.go:158 +0x63
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

A running container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

podman appears to run correctly and not panic in the following circumstances:

1. Running the example command above as root
2. Running the example command above as non-root but with --network host

So it seems related to not having slirp4netns installed on Debian and running in rootless mode. The error is visible with current git master 3ed265c5: "Merge pull request #4104 from mheon/bump-1.6.0-rc2".

**Output of `podman version`:**

I can't quite see why the "unable to set namespace" permission error is happening? Certainly both kernel.unprivileged_userns_clone and kernel.unprivileged_userns_apparmor_policy are currently set to 1.

Interesting - we're not able to join the rootless pause process because of an EPERM...

Can you try a `podman system migrate` and reset again.

As the original reporter it looks like there are 2 separate but related issues here:

1) SIGSEGV running rootless podman when slirp4netns is not installed due to NULL pointer reference (this is the original stack trace posted above)

2) EPERM issue trying to set netns in rootless podman spotted by @mheon in the debug output above

I followed the suggestion by @afbjorklund above to revert `5813c82` and I can confirm this appears to fix both issues for me.
opened a PR to take care of both issues: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4139
